designSystem.styleGuidelines:
## Brand & Style
The design system is built upon a concept of visual duality, reflecting the "Good and Evil" narrative of a premium Little Rock smokehouse. It juxtaposes the ethereal, communal "Good" (light, gold, airy layouts) against the intense, charred "Evil" (dark, smoke-filled, high-contrast typography). 

The style is **High-End Editorial**. It avoids traditional "rustic" BBQ clichés in favor of a sophisticated, literary aesthetic found in premium food journals. The interface uses a "Split" motif—clean vertical or horizontal divisions—to showcase the two sides of the culinary experience. Subtle halftone textures are applied to imagery to evoke traditional print techniques and woodblock history.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Fixed Grid** philosophy on desktop with generous, unconventional margins to mimic a high-end magazine. 

*   **Duality Split:** Use a 50/50 vertical split on large screens to contrast content types (e.g., "The Good" ingredients vs. "The Evil" techniques).
*   **Whitespace:** Negative space is treated as a design element, not "empty" space. Large section gaps (120px+) prevent the high-contrast colors from feeling cluttered.
*   **Asymmetry:** Elements should occasionally break the grid or overlap boundaries to create a modern, curated feel.

## Elevation & Depth
This design system rejects standard shadows in favor of **Tonal Layering** and **High-Contrast Outlines**.

*   **Flat Depth:** Hierarchy is established through color blocking rather than blur. Elements are "raised" by switching from Charred Black to Smoke Gray surfaces.
*   **Hard Outlines:** Use 1px solid borders in Gold or Bone White to define interactive containers. 
*   **Glassmorphism (Smoke):** A subtle backdrop-blur (12px) with a 20% opacity black fill is used for navigation overlays to simulate looking through a haze of smoke.

## Components
Consistent styling across the interface:

*   **Buttons:** Primary buttons are solid Divine Gold with Black text, rectangular, and use the `label-bold` type style. Secondary buttons are "Ghost" style with a 1px Bone White border.
*   **Menu Cards:** Use a split layout—item name in Bodoni Moda on the left, price/description in Hanken Grotesk on the right, separated by a thin 1px horizontal rule.
*   **Chips/Tags:** Used for dietary labels (e.g., "Spicy", "GF"). Small, rectangular boxes with Bone White text on a Smoke Gray background.
*   **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only (1px Bone White) with floating labels in Hanken Grotesk to maintain a minimal, editorial look.
*   **Halftone Dividers:** Instead of solid lines, use a recurring halftone gradient pattern to transition between "Good" and "Evil" sections.

name: Duality & Embers
colors:

  on-primary-container: '#554300'
  inverse-primary: '#735c00'

  secondary-container: '#4a4949'
  on-secondary-container: '#bab8b7'

  on-primary-fixed-variant: '#574500'
  secondary-fixed: '#e5e2e1'
  secondary-fixed-dim: '#c9c6c5'
  on-secondary-fixed: '#1c1b1b'
  on-secondary-fixed-variant: '#474646'
  tertiary-fixed: '#e2e2e2'
  tertiary-fixed-dim: '#c6c6c7'
  on-tertiary-fixed: '#1a1c1c'
  on-tertiary-fixed-variant: '#454747'
  background: '#131313'
  on-background: '#e4e2e1'
  surface-variant: '#353535'
typography:
  display-lg:
    fontFamily: Bodoni Moda
    fontSize: 72px
    fontWeight: '800'
    lineHeight: '1.1'
    letterSpacing: -0.02em
  display-lg-mobile:
    fontFamily: Bodoni Moda
    fontSize: 48px

    fontFamily: Bodoni Moda

  headline-lg:

  container-max: 1280px
  gutter: 24px
  margin-mobile: 16px
  margin-desktop: 64px
  section-gap: 120px
---

## Brand & Style
The design system is built upon a concept of visual duality, reflecting the "Good and Evil" narrative of a premium Little Rock smokehouse. It juxtaposes the ethereal, communal "Good" (light, gold, airy layouts) against the intense, charred "Evil" (dark, smoke-filled, high-contrast typography). 

The style is **High-End Editorial**. It avoids traditional "rustic" BBQ clichés in favor of a sophisticated, literary aesthetic found in premium food journals. The interface uses a "Split" motif—clean vertical or horizontal divisions—to showcase the two sides of the culinary experience. Subtle halftone textures are applied to imagery to evoke traditional print techniques and woodblock history.

## Colors
The palette is a high-contrast study in light and shadow. 

*   **Charred Black (#0D0D0D):** The foundation. Used for backgrounds to represent the "Evil" side—intensity, smoke, and the char of the pit.
*   **Bone White (#F5F5F5):** The "Good" side. Used for high-readability canvases, evoking the purity of ingredients and communal light.
*   **Divine Gold (#D4AF37):** A premium accent used sparingly for iconography, borders, and call-to-actions, representing the "heavenly" flavor and the glow of embers.
*   **Smoke Gray (#333333):** Used for secondary text, subtle dividers, and depth-building in dark mode.

The design system utilizes a "Hard-Switch" approach where sections are either predominantly Bone White with Black text or Charred Black with Gold/White text, reinforcing the duality.

## Typography
Typography is the primary vehicle for the brand’s authoritative voice. 

**Bodoni Moda** is used for all headlines. Its high contrast between thick and thin strokes provides a classic editorial feel that mirrors the "cut" of a blade and the precision of the smokehouse. 

**Hanken Grotesk** serves as the utilitarian balance. It is clean, contemporary, and highly legible, ensuring that menu descriptions and logistics remain accessible despite the bold aesthetic.

Hierarchy is enforced through extreme scale—large display serifs paired with small, tracked-out uppercase sans-serif labels.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Fixed Grid** philosophy on desktop with generous, unconventional margins to mimic a high-end magazine. 

*   **Duality Split:** Use a 50/50 vertical split on large screens to contrast content types (e.g., "The Good" ingredients vs. "The Evil" techniques).
*   **Whitespace:** Negative space is treated as a design element, not "empty" space. Large section gaps (120px+) prevent the high-contrast colors from feeling cluttered.
*   **Asymmetry:** Elements should occasionally break the grid or overlap boundaries to create a modern, curated feel.

## Elevation & Depth
This design system rejects standard shadows in favor of **Tonal Layering** and **High-Contrast Outlines**.

*   **Flat Depth:** Hierarchy is established through color blocking rather than blur. Elements are "raised" by switching from Charred Black to Smoke Gray surfaces.
*   **Hard Outlines:** Use 1px solid borders in Gold or Bone White to define interactive containers. 
*   **Glassmorphism (Smoke):** A subtle backdrop-blur (12px) with a 20% opacity black fill is used for navigation overlays to simulate looking through a haze of smoke.

## Shapes
The shape language is **Sharp (0)**. 

To maintain the authoritative and "cut" nature of the brand, no rounded corners are permitted. Buttons, input fields, images, and cards must all utilize 90-degree angles. This geometric rigidity reinforces the professional, "no-cliché" editorial stance of this design system.

## Components
Consistent styling across the interface:

*   **Buttons:** Primary buttons are solid Divine Gold with Black text, rectangular, and use the `label-bold` type style. Secondary buttons are "Ghost" style with a 1px Bone White border.
*   **Menu Cards:** Use a split layout—item name in Bodoni Moda on the left, price/description in Hanken Grotesk on the right, separated by a thin 1px horizontal rule.
*   **Chips/Tags:** Used for dietary labels (e.g., "Spicy", "GF"). Small, rectangular boxes with Bone White text on a Smoke Gray background.
*   **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only (1px Bone White) with floating labels in Hanken Grotesk to maintain a minimal, editorial look.
*   **Halftone Dividers:** Instead of solid lines, use a recurring halftone gradient pattern to transition between "Good" and "Evil" sections.

screens.prompt: A high-end, editorial photograph of premium smoked brisket being sliced. The lighting is cinematic and low-key, highlighting the "smoke ring" and the glistening texture of the meat against a charred black background. Divine gold light catching the smoke. No people. 8k resolution, professional food photography.

The lighting is cinematic and low-key, highlighting the "smoke ring" and the glistening texture of the meat against a charred black background. Divine gold light catching the smoke. No people. 8k resolution, professional food photography.

screens.prompt: A soulful, warm photograph of a weathered wooden BBQ pit in Little Rock, Arkansas at sunrise. Soft, divine gold light filtering through wood smoke. Atmospheric, community-focused, nostalgic but premium. 8k resolution.

screens.name: projects/9132108788539206898/screens/051007def5774872b2f19078007c6b57
screens.prompt:
Build the complete desktop homepage for Good and Evil BBQ. Use the 'Duality & Embers' design system (dark mode, Bodoni Moda headlines). Layout strategy: Editorial duality. High contrast between light (Good) and dark (Evil) sections. Sections: 1. Header: Fixed with logo {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_2}}, phone (501) 214-8040, and 'Order Now' button. 2. Hero: Cinematic split-screen or large immersive layout using {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_5}} (The Evil - Charred Brisket). Headline: "The Sacred Art of Smoke." 3. Credibility: "Little Rock's Best Kept Secret" - subtle section with texture and community-focused copy. 4. Services/Offerings: Use {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_4}} (The Good - Community Pit) to contrast. Showcase brisket, ribs, and community catering. 5. About: Narrative story of Eric Love and the Little Rock roots. 6. Visit: Section with hours and the required empty map slot:
7. Contact: Minimal form with validation UI. 8. Footer: Complete details as per instructions. Technical: Smooth scroll, mobile-responsive nav, premium feel. Character-for-character reproduction of contact info: 2616 Springer Boulevard, Little Rock, AR 72206. Email: ericlove816@Yahoo.com. Phone: (501) 214-8040. ## Available Images You MUST include the following images in this design. You must reference them by putting their exact placeholder IDs in `src` attributes or CSS `background-image` rules. Do NOT use external URLs or generate new placeholders. Preserve these IDs exactly as written. Read each image's Description to understand its subject, intended section, and visual role. - {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_2}} Alt Text: Good and Evil BBQ Logo Description: Brand logo. - Primary color: #d4af37 - Font: bodoniModa - Mode: dark - Roundness: rounded-lg Dimensions: 200x80 Aspect Ratio: 2.50 Colors: #333333, #D4AF37, #F5F5F5

The style is **High-End Editorial**. It avoids traditional "rustic" BBQ clichés in favor of a sophisticated, literary aesthetic found in premium food journals. The interface uses a "Split" motif—clean vertical or horizontal divisions—to showcase the two sides of the culinary experience. Subtle halftone textures are applied to imagery to evoke traditional print techniques and woodblock history.

## Colors
The palette is a high-contrast study in light and shadow. 

*   **Charred Black (#0D0D0D):** The foundation. Used for backgrounds to represent the "Evil" side—intensity, smoke, and the char of the pit.
*   **Bone White (#F5F5F5):** The "Good" side. Used for high-readability canvases, evoking the purity of ingredients and communal light.
*   **Divine Gold (#D4AF37):** A premium accent used sparingly for iconography, borders, and call-to-actions, representing the "heavenly" flavor and the glow of embers.
*   **Smoke Gray (#333333):** Used for secondary text, subtle dividers, and depth-building in dark mode.

The design system utilizes a "Hard-Switch" approach where sections are either predominantly Bone White with Black text or Charred Black with Gold/White text, reinforcing the duality.

## Typography
Typography is the primary vehicle for the brand’s authoritative voice. 

**Bodoni Moda** is used for all headlines. Its high contrast between thick and thin strokes provides a classic editorial feel that mirrors the "cut" of a blade and the precision of the smokehouse. 

**Hanken Grotesk** serves as the utilitarian balance. It is clean, contemporary, and highly legible, ensuring that menu descriptions and logistics remain accessible despite the bold aesthetic.

Hierarchy is enforced through extreme scale—large display serifs paired with small, tracked-out uppercase sans-serif labels.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Fixed Grid** philosophy on desktop with generous, unconventional margins to mimic a high-end magazine. 

*   **Duality Split:** Use a 50/50 vertical split on large screens to contrast content types (e.g., "The Good" ingredients vs. "The Evil" techniques).
*   **Whitespace:** Negative space is treated as a design element, not "empty" space. Large section gaps (120px+) prevent the high-contrast colors from feeling cluttered.
*   **Asymmetry:** Elements should occasionally break the grid or overlap boundaries to create a modern, curated feel.

## Elevation & Depth
This design system rejects standard shadows in favor of **Tonal Layering** and **High-Contrast Outlines**.

*   **Flat Depth:** Hierarchy is established through color blocking rather than blur. Elements are "raised" by switching from Charred Black to Smoke Gray surfaces.
*   **Hard Outlines:** Use 1px solid borders in Gold or Bone White to define interactive containers. 
*   **Glassmorphism (Smoke):** A subtle backdrop-blur (12px) with a 20% opacity black fill is used for navigation overlays to simulate looking through a haze of smoke.

## Shapes
The shape language is **Sharp (0)**. 

To maintain the authoritative and "cut" nature of the brand, no rounded corners are permitted. Buttons, input fields, images, and cards must all utilize 90-degree angles. This geometric rigidity reinforces the professional, "no-cliché" editorial stance of this design system.

## Components
Consistent styling across the interface:

*   **Buttons:** Primary buttons are solid Divine Gold with Black text, rectangular, and use the `label-bold` type style. Secondary buttons are "Ghost" style with a 1px Bone White border.
*   **Menu Cards:** Use a split layout—item name in Bodoni Moda on the left, price/description in Hanken Grotesk on the right, separated by a thin 1px horizontal rule.
*   **Chips/Tags:** Used for dietary labels (e.g., "Spicy", "GF"). Small, rectangular boxes with Bone White text on a Smoke Gray background.
*   **Input Fields:** Bottom-border only (1px Bone White) with floating labels in Hanken Grotesk to maintain a minimal, editorial look.
*   **Halftone Dividers:** Instead of solid lines, use a recurring halftone gradient pattern to transition between "Good" and "Evil" sections.

screens.title: Good and Evil BBQ - Homepage

text:
I've designed a bespoke, editorial-grade homepage for **Good and Evil BBQ** that captures the unique duality of their brand: the soulful, community spirit of Little Rock ("The Good") and the intense, charred mastery of the pit ("The Evil").

### Design Highlights:
*   **Visual Duality:** The layout uses a high-contrast theme, moving between deep charred blacks and warm bone whites, accented by "Divine Gold" to reflect both the grit and the premium quality of the smokehouse.
*   **Editorial Layout:** I’ve moved away from standard grids in favor of an asymmetric, narrative-driven structure that feels like a premium food magazine.
*   **Atmospheric Photography:** I generated custom, cinematic imagery including a low-key study of glistening smoked brisket and a warm, nostalgic scene of the Little Rock community pit at sunrise.
*   **Authentic Typography:** Headlines use the bold, authoritative **Bodoni Moda** serif, providing a sophisticated slab-serif feel that balances the industrial nature of BBQ with a high-end dining experience.
*   **Functional Polish:** The site includes a fixed navigation bar for easy access to ordering and contact info, a responsive mobile menu, and a custom-styled slot for your map visualization.

The complete production-ready frontend code is now active on your canvas.

suggestion: Make the 'The Evil' section even darker and more atmospheric
Add a smooth fade-in animation to the hero headline
Refine the menu section to look like a high-end printed card
